title: Design transcription-quality attribution workflow for feedback misclassifications

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Initial feasibility scan in progress.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Feasibility findings: existing FeedbackAnalysis already computes mismatches and writes per-cell indexed JSON with feedback/item IDs and item identifiers. MCP feedback_find already returns item_text + item_identifiers and supports initial/final value filtering, so a transcription-attribution workflow is possible without schema changes. Next: recommend report-vs-procedure architecture and output contract for reportId/formId lists.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Implementation started: creating initial procedure YAML to identify likely transcription-caused mismatches and output form/report IDs with confidence reasons.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: User clarified QA comments are not useful for attribution; implementation will rely on transcript text heuristics (entity presence/absence) on mismatch items.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Implemented initial procedure: plexus/procedures/transcription_mismatch_attribution.yaml. It pages plexus_feedback_find, evaluates transcript-only missing-entity/missing-intro heuristics, and outputs form/report/session IDs with confidence + reasons.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: New requirement: expected names vary per call. Updating procedure to extract expected names from item metadata and apply fuzzy transcript matching; company fixed to Renewal by Andersen for this scorecard.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Refinement requested: remove broad metadata heuristics. Use explicit item metadata key agent_name (e.g., Manuel Valverde), derive first/last from that, and fuzzy-match transcript text. Company remains Renewal by Andersen.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Implemented requested simplification in transcription_mismatch_attribution procedure: expected names now come from item_metadata.agent_name (or agentName) per call, split to first/last, and matched with fuzzy token similarity. Set expected_company default to 'Renewal by Andersen'. Added tunable name_similarity_threshold (default 0.8).

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Starting pilot run requested by user: transcription_mismatch_attribution for scorecard 731 and score 47088 with small max_items cap.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Attempted to run procedure via CLI but blocked by runtime error: TactusRuntime.__init__ unexpected keyword argument trace_sink. Proceeding with one-off script execution of equivalent logic for user-requested pilot run (scorecard 731, score 47088).

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Pilot execution (workaround script) for scorecard 731 / score 47088 over last 30 days: resolved scorecard_id=2d3970b9-47a3-423b-9ad1-4e103d9136c8, score_id=ae02921d-3cf3-49e7-a099-bc79b6478023. Retrieved 79 FN items (No->Yes), flagged 78 as likely transcription-related using agent_name + company heuristics. Observed item identifiers mostly lacked formId/reportId/sessionId (null in sample output).

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Investigating procedure runner failure: TactusRuntime.__init__ unexpected keyword argument trace_sink. Plan is to patch executor for runtime signature compatibility and re-validate with dry run.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Patched procedure executor for Tactus compatibility: runtime kwargs are now signature-filtered (trace_sink only when supported) and agent tools normalization now handles mixed lists containing plexus_* plus done/think.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Validated runner now advances past trace_sink and toolset resolution. Found additional runtime/API drift in procedure script: DSPyAgentHandle lacks reset(). Updated transcription_mismatch_attribution procedure to guard reset call (only call if method exists).

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Additional compatibility fixes: default ProcedureMetadata.status when API returns null; made ProcedureChatRecorder.end_session accept both legacy (status-first) and new Tactus (session_id-first) call styles.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Running full procedure execution test with temporary YAML param values for scorecard 731 / score 47088 and small max_items cap.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Re-test after compatibility patches: runner now initializes and executes Tactus workflow. New blocker surfaced in MCP bridge: tool wrapper rejects keyword args ( call fails with unexpected keyword argument 'days'). Also CLI  output currently crashes on non-serializable  in result payload.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Re-test after compatibility patches: runner now initializes and executes Tactus workflow. New blocker surfaced in MCP bridge: tool wrapper rejects keyword args on plexus_feedback_find (unexpected keyword argument days). Also CLI JSON output crashes on non-serializable LuaTable values in result payload.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: User requested full proper fix (no hacks). Implementing upstream compatibility in Tactus MCP adapter + Plexus procedure output serialization, then validating with real run for scorecard 731 / score 47088.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Starting no-hacks fix pass: patching Tactus MCP adapter kwargs/signature compatibility and Plexus procedure JSON serialization for LuaTable outputs, then validating with scorecard 731 / score 47088 run.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Implemented no-hacks compatibility fixes across Plexus+Tactus and validated with live run. Tactus MCP adapter now accepts kwargs/legacy args, coerces scalar->string for string-typed MCP schemas, and unwraps MCP content payloads into plain text for Lua JSON decoding. Plexus procedure CLI JSON output now recursively normalizes non-serializable Lua-like/model objects. Validation: Tactus adapter tests 24 passed; Plexus procedure compat/tests 10 passed + JSON-safe tests. Live run with patched runtime (PYTHONPATH=/home/derek/projects/Tactus) and YAML defaults scorecard=731 score=47088 completed successfully: procedure_id=0ece1e3b-edae-4ce9-bc53-cd007a327a8e task_id=9ba2ffc3-f3be-4a36-9fa0-39a998487e2e, scanned=79 mismatches=79 likely_transcription_issue_count=78 rate=0.98734.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: User-reported false missing last-name on hyphenated surname (Nunez-Tovar) where transcript contains Nunez. Patching transcription attribution procedure name-matching to handle hyphenated last-name components robustly and validating against sample.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Patched transcription_mismatch_attribution name matching for compound/hyphenated names: full token match is attempted first, then component token fallback. Last-name check now allows partial component match (e.g., Nunez-Tovar matches transcript token Nunez). Validation run confirms item external_id 305359138 now only flags missing_expected_first_name (missing_expected_last_name removed).

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Adding per-agent attribution breakdown to transcription_mismatch_attribution output: mismatch_count, likely_transcription_issue_count, and rate per agent to identify concentrated transcription quality issues.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Implemented per-agent attribution outputs in transcription_mismatch_attribution: agent_breakdown_by_agent and sorted agent_breakdown with mismatch_count, likely_transcription_issue_count, likely_transcription_issue_rate, confidence_counts, likely_points_total. Verified via run output (/tmp/transcription_run_output_agent_breakdown.log). Example concentrations: Damaria Shezbie 13/13, Anais Nunez-Tovar 13/13, Colleen Colvin 11/12, Apr Seaberry Hickman 7/7, Mia Carriuolo 6/6.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Adding stable form-id extraction to transcription attribution output so each mismatch has lookup-friendly form_id. Priority: item_identifiers.formId -> metadata form fields/content_id -> external_id fallback, with form_id_source for traceability.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Corrected ID semantics per user feedback: procedure no longer labels report/content IDs as form_id. form_id is now emitted only when true form identifiers exist in item_identifiers/item metadata form keys. Added lookup_id/lookup_id_source as explicit fallback for manual lookup. Validation run shows lookup_id populated (mostly item_metadata.content_id) and form_id absent for this dataset, confirming form IDs are not present in current source records.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Investigating why procedure output has null identifier fields despite dashboard item detail showing Form Id/Report Id. Next steps: trace feedback_find query path and item identifier hydration, then patch backend to expose true identifiers (especially form_id) in procedure inputs.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Fixed null identifier output in feedback_find/procedure path. Root cause: feedback service/model path only relied on legacy item.identifiers and did not preserve modern item.itemIdentifiers relationship used by dashboard item detail views. Changes: (1) FeedbackItem.from_dict now normalizes item.itemIdentifiers.items -> item.identifiers when legacy identifiers are absent. (2) FeedbackService GSI + fallback queries now request item.itemIdentifiers fields. (3) FeedbackService identifier normalization/extraction now handles list/dict/string payloads consistently and recognizes form/form id/formId/form_id names. Validation: pytest tests/test_feedback_service.py (24 passed); live procedure run output now includes item_identifiers and form_id values (e.g., form_id 66919927 for item ...--305359138).

comments.author: derek
comments.text: User requested larger run. Updating /tmp run YAML to target up to 1000 feedback samples and executing procedure with PYTHONPATH pointed at Tactus.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Replacing ad-hoc /tmp run YAML with committed reusable run config: plexus/procedures/runs/transcription_mismatch_attribution_731_47088_1000.yaml (params include scorecard 731, score 47088, days 365, max_items 1000).

comments.author: derek
comments.text: User requested permanent procedure YAML in root procedures directory. Moved run-ready file to plexus/procedures/transcription_mismatch_attribution_731_47088_1000.yaml and removed intermediate runs/ wrapper file.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Correction: policy blocked file move/delete operations. I copied the run-ready file to plexus/procedures/transcription_mismatch_attribution_731_47088_1000.yaml in the root procedures directory. Intermediate files remain under plexus/procedures/runs for now.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: User reported regression: 1000-sample run fails with TaskGroup sub-exception. Investigating exact stack/error in /tmp/transcription_run_output_1000.log and applying a real fix.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Resolved 1000-run failure. Root cause: Tactus DSPyAgentHandle lacked legacy-compatible reset(), so pagination loop in procedure could not clear fetcher conversation between pages; second agent turn accumulated prior large tool-response history and failed with transient TaskGroup errors. Fixes in Tactus: (1) add DSPyAgentHandle.reset() alias to clear_history(), (2) add transient retry classification + retries for MCP tool execution callback in tactus/adapters/mcp_manager.py. Validation: Tactus tests pass (adapters/test_mcp_manager.py:14 passed; dspy/test_agent_execution targeted reset tests passed). Re-ran procedure YAML plexus/procedures/transcription_mismatch_attribution_731_47088_1000.yaml successfully; output status=completed, total_feedback_items_scanned=184, total_mismatches_scanned=184, likely_transcription_issue_count=181.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: User requested new-procedure path use new agent API instead of legacy-only reset. Updating transcription_mismatch_attribution to prefer clear_history() and keep reset() only as fallback for older runtimes.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: User reports continued TaskGroup failures; investigating latest failing run c39cca82 session / procedure 9870106d and implementing deterministic reliability fix.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Identified direct regression cause: Lua type-check around Fetcher.clear_history/reset treated Python-bound methods as non-function, so history reset was skipped between pages. Fixed in both procedure YAMLs by direct pcall(Fetcher.clear_history()) with reset() fallback. Validation run now stable: completed with two page fetches (two plexus_feedback_find tool executions), total_feedback_items_scanned=184, total_mismatches_scanned=184, likely_transcription_issue_count=182.
